step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with option C because immediate surgical intervention is essential to salvage the involved testis before necrosis occurs. Additionally, performing surgery on the contralateral side even if asymptomatic helps reduce future risk of torsion by removing potential anatomical vulnerabilities such as the presence of an "anteriorly displaced" spermatic cord – which can predispose to torsion regardless of symptoms.

Final answer: C
